texto: I have made bouquets of pleats, bouquets of flowers, bouquets of ruffles, bouquets of feathers. Often I design in mousseline, held tightly around the waist, and with something else going on all around.
explicacao: Citação de Giambattista Valli sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “I have made bouquets of pleats, bouquets of flowers, bouquets of ruffles, bouquets of feathers. O…” articula reflexao, flowers, feathers em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como feathers e going.

seo.titleSeo: "I have made bouquets of pleats, bouquets of flowers,…" — Giambattista Valli | Metamensagem
seo.descriptionSeo: Citação de Giambattista Valli sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “I have made bouquets of pleats, bouquets of flowers, bouquets of ruffles, bouquets of feathers.…

frase_original: I have made bouquets of pleats, bouquets of flowers, bouquets of ruffles, bouquets of feathers. Often I design in mousseline, held tightly around the waist, and with something else going on all around.

texto: I like things that are kind of eclectic, when one thing doesn't go with another. That's why I love Rome. The town itself is that way. It's where Fascist architecture meets classic Renaissance, where the ancient bangs up against the contemporary. It has a touch of everything. That's my style, and that's what my work is about.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Giambattista Valli: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “I like things that are kind of eclectic, when one thing doesn't go with another. That's why I lov…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que tempo, love, work pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de trabalho e work.

frase_original: I like things that are kind of eclectic, when one thing doesn't go with another. That's why I love Rome. The town itself is that way. It's where Fascist architecture meets classic Renaissance, where the ancient bangs up against the contemporary. It has a touch of everything. That's my style, and that's what my work is about.
